action.reasoning: Observation: the corrected 0.33/0.22/0.45 ensemble is a new best at balanced_accuracy 0.6594, improving over 0.30/0.20/0.50 by reducing the seed-123 weight while preserving its complementary bobcat/cat/empty gains. Analysis: the trend suggests that moving a bit further toward the original 4e/10e ensemble may recover bird/dog/squirrel/rabbit recall, though too little seed-123 may lose its key gains. A cheap nearby sweep is much lower risk than new training and costs about 1 minute. Decision: evaluate 60% previous 4e/10e mixture plus 40% seed-123, i.e. weights 0.36/0.24/0.40, using the known-working ensemble checkpoint schema.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ck=torch.load('checkpoints/dino_ens_4_10_s123_w332245_fixed.pt', map_location='cpu'); ck['ensemble']=[{'checkpoint':'checkpoints/dino_4e.pt','weight':0.36},{'checkpoint':'checkpoints/dino_10e.pt','weight':0.24},{'checkpoint':'checkpoints/dino_4e_s123.pt','weight':0.40}]; ck['name']='dino_ens_4_10_s123_w362440'; ck['source_checkpoint']='modified_from_dino_ens_4_10_s123_w332245_fixed'; torch.save(ck, 'checkpoints/dino_ens_4_10_s123_w362440.pt'); print('saved checkpoints/dino_ens_4_10_s123_w362440.pt'); print(ck['ensemble'])"
